key_hits: Correctly identifies VIC Health Club and weekly schedule
Captures all 11 classes with correct categories and most subtitles
Correctly notes URL vichealth.club, QR code, legend colors
key_misses: Wrong end times: OSTEOPOROSIS '13:10-14:10' (should be 14:00), Tue TAIJI '13:10-14:10'
Wed/Thu YOGA listed as '13:30-14:00' (should be 13:00-14:00) — three time errors
hallucinations: OSTEOPOROSIS icon described as 'person with a walking stick' (actually standing from squat)
judgments.justification: Good structural coverage of all 11 classes but multiple timing OCR errors and one dropped letter in QIGONG indicate weak text precision.

key_hits: All 11 classes with correct day, time, color category, and subtitles
Correct legend (blue/orange/green = Yoga/Shaolin/Fitness)
Correct URL and QR code
Recognizes faint lotus flower background
key_misses: Logo described as 'hands or wings' holding heart — slightly vague
judgments.justification: Comprehensive and highly accurate transcription with only minor descriptive imprecision on the logo.

Correct URL and footer
Correctly identifies logo as 'hands cradling heart in laurel wreath'
Calls out icon set accurately
judgments.justification: Best-in-class verbatim transcription; every class, time, and detail matches ground truth exactly.

ranking: gemma4-26b-a4b|think (9) - all correct, minor logo wording
qwen3-vl-4b|think (9) - all correct for a small model
qwen3.5-9b|nothink (8) - all correct + QR globe hallucination
qwen3.6-27b|nothink (8) - all correct + QR globe hallucination
qwen3.6-27b|think (8) - all correct + QR globe hallucination
qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink (8) - all correct + QR globe hallucination
qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think (8) - all correct + QR globe hallucination
gemma4-12b|think (7) - timing OCR errors and QIGONG letter drop
notes: Most models handled this dense text-heavy schedule very well — 14 of 15 captured all 11 classes with correct color categories.
Ground-truth-specified KEY FACTS list (yoga, shaolin, fitness, qigong, taiji, calisthenics) all appeared correctly in nearly all responses.
A recurring hallucination across the entire qwen3.6 family (both think and nothink) is a fabricated 'globe icon in the center of the QR code' — this is consistent but ungrounded.
Only gemma4-12b|think had meaningful data errors: wrong end times on 3 classes and 'IGONG' missing the Q.
gemma4-31b|think was the clear winner with perfect verbatim transcription including correct logo and icons.
The OSTEOPOROSIS icon (person standing from a squat) was consistently mislabeled as 'lifting weights' or 'walking stick' across multiple models — a minor icon-recognition weakness.
